source: FII Institute — Rise of the Gigafirm: next trillion-dollar tech company (panel)
summary: GPU utilization rate is the key bottleneck and investment thesis in AI infrastructure
summary_extended: Baker argues that GPU utilization rate — currently around 30% — is the central bottleneck in AI infrastructure, and that GPUs consume full power even when idle, making low utilization extremely costly and wasteful. He contends that investments in storage, networking, and memory technologies that raise GPU utilization from ~30% to ~60% effectively double the output of an AI factory without adding a single additional GPU. Baker describes this as his and his co-panelist's 'shared core infrastructure level thesis,' indicating high conviction and actual portfolio positioning around it. The investment implication is to own companies in the storage, high-speed networking, and memory subsystems that enable higher GPU utilization — not necessarily the GPU vendors themselves. No specific company tickers were named or clearly implied with enough specificity to add unambiguously, as the thesis spans multiple subsectors without naming particular beneficiaries.
detail: GPUs are idle ~70% of the time, consuming full power while doing nothing. Investments in storage, networking, and memory technologies that increase GPU utilization from ~30% to ~60% effectively double AI factory output. This is the core infrastructure investment thesis Baker and his co-panelist share.
confidence_evidence: Baker describes this as their 'shared core infrastructure level thesis' with specific utilization statistics and multiple shared portfolio investments
quote_fragment: investing in things that increase the GPU utilization rate
